description: An aspect particle that marks actions as complete wholes, viewed from outside as finished events. More than indicating completion, 'ki' acknowledges the satisfaction of boundaries—the way some experiences need to be understood as unified events rather than ongoing processes.
sound_symbolism: The particle begins with 'k', a sharp velar stop that creates clear acoustic boundaries—the back of the tongue briefly blocking airflow completely before release. This decisive consonant meets 'i', the highest, most focused vowel, like a pin marking a precise point. Together, 'ki' sounds like completion itself: sharp, clear, definitive. The sound requires full closure and clean release, training speakers to recognize when actions form complete gestalts rather than flowing processes.
grammatical_notes: Occupies Slot 1, appearing after tense markers but before voice/modality particles. Combines with tenses to create specific temporal-aspectual meanings: 'to ki' (had completed), 'so ki' (will have completed). Unmarked verbs are aspectually neutral, making perfective explicitly marked and conscious. Views events from outside, as bounded wholes—contrasting with imperfective 'si' which views events from within. Often used for achievements, accomplishments, and any action conceived as a complete unit.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The particle embodies the teaching that some experiences must be allowed their completion—like meditation sessions that have beginning and end, or the full cycle of breath. 'Ki' acknowledges that wisdom includes knowing when something is finished.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Like the clear boundaries of Art Nouveau design elements—where each leaf, each curve has definite form—'ki' creates linguistic gestalts. It allows actions to achieve the aesthetic completeness of finished artworks.
